name: Zenfeed
what_it_does: Zenfeed is an AI-powered RSS aggregator that processes feeds through configurable LLM pipelines to score, classify, filter, and summarize content, with MCP server integration, email notifications, daily briefs, and open query/export APIs.
use_cases: Replacing traditional RSS readers with an AI-curated feed that scores and filters articles by relevance
Receiving AI-generated daily email briefs summarizing your subscribed RSS sources
Building a personal AI search engine over trusted RSS sources with fast query APIs
Monitoring specific topics or events across RSS feeds with automated analysis reports
not_for: Users who prefer reading full unfiltered feeds without AI intermediation
Deployments requiring user-facing authentication (no built-in auth)
Teams needing a managed SaaS solution rather than self-hosted Docker
best_when: You subscribe to many RSS feeds and want AI to filter noise, surface relevant content, and deliver concise daily summaries instead of an overwhelming unread count.
avoid_when: You need multi-user support, built-in authentication, or want to avoid LLM API costs for content processing.

auth.notes: No built-in authentication mechanism. Publicly documented as a security concern if deployed on public internet. Requires LLM API keys (defaults to SiliconFlow/Qwen models).

pricing.notes: AGPLv3 open source. Default uses Qwen/Qwen3-8B (free tier via SiliconFlow). LLM API costs vary by provider and feed volume. Data retained 8 days by default.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Go-based service; error handling likely structured but not documented in detail for MCP consumers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Feed ingestion is naturally idempotent (same articles won't be duplicated), but this is not explicitly documented.
known_agent_gotchas: No built-in authentication — must not be exposed publicly without a reverse proxy with auth
LLM API keys required; cost scales with feed volume and processing complexity
Data retention is only 8 days by default — agents cannot query historical content beyond this window
AGPLv3 license requires source disclosure for network-deployed modifications
Default port 1400 (web) and 1301 (MCP) — both must be secured in production
